def: part. adj. Ruined, demolished, destroyed

subs: v.t. To ruin, demolish, raze, destroy.

Mouth; face, countenance; aspect; presence; — aperture, orifice, opening; inlet, outlet; — source; — respect, regard, esteem; complaisance; partiality, bias; — strength, power; capacity, ability, fitness, qualification; pretension; pretence; — demand, charge, price (e.g. us wakīl-kā baṛā muṅh hai)
subs: (-kā), The face to become shrunken, or reduced, or drawn, or thin
or muṅh uṭhāʼe, adv. With the face up, or turned upwards; without looking before one; blindly; head-foremost, headlong
adv. On waking, or rising (from bed)
v.n. 'The face to be bright or white,' not to incur disgrace or dishonour; to come off with credit, or with flying colours
adj. & adv. By word of mouth, verbal; — verbally (syn. zabānī)
or muṅhāṅ-muṅh, adj. Face to face; to one's face; — brimful (syn. lab-ā-lab); — muṅhā-muṅh bharnā, v.t. To fill to the brim; to stuff; to overstock
(-ko), To censure (one) to his face; — to slap (one) on the face
(-kā), To be or become salivated; — to have the thrush; — to utter taunts (against, -par); — s.m. Salivation
adj. (f. -ī), Dark, obscure; — s.m. Dusk, twilight; evening; dawn of day, daybreak; — muṅh-andhere, adv. Before daybreak; at dawn
To open the mouth, to gape; — to give a forced laugh; — to expire
'To sit with the mouth bound up or closed,' to sit silent
To make a wry face, to look displeased or sour, to frown or scowl

(apnā, or kisī-kā), To make a wry face, to frown, scowl; to look sulky, to pout; — to make faces; — to spoil or disfigure the face (of another); to put one out of countenance; — to leave a bad taste in the mouth
v.n. 'The face to be soured,' to be displeased; — to have the taste blunted or depraved; — to have a bad taste in the mouth
To fashion the face (of, -kā); — to make faces (at), to mock; — to express feeling through the features
s.m. A muzzle; — adj. With the mouth closed
To hold one's tongue; — to stop the mouth (of, -kā), to silence; to give hush-money (to), to bribe (i.q. muṅh bharnā)
s.m. pl. A sect of Sewṛās who keep a cloth hanging before their mouths
To make (oneself), or to put on, a good face; — to fit or qualify oneself (for)
adj. (f. -bolī), Nominal, in name or appellation, so-called; adopted
s.m., or muṇh-bolī bahan, s.f. A brother, or a sister, so-called, an adopted brother or sister; an intimate friend
v.n. The month to water; — to feel nausea, to be squeamish
s.f. Hush-money, a bribe, a sop
adv. 'Filling the mouth'; fully, completely, thoroughly (used by women; cf. peṭ-bhar-ke)
(-kā), To fill the mouth (of); to stop the mouth (of) with a sop, to give hush-money (to); to bribe
(-kā), To find (one) favourably disposed; to get into the good graces (of); to presume on the favour (of)
adv. On or upon (one's) mouth or face; — before the face, in the presence (of, -ke, or mere, &c.)
(-ke), To spit on the face (of); — (fig̠.) to disgrace, or insult
(-ke), To be restrained through respect or regard (for another)
'Not to put a grain to the mouth,' to go without a morsel of food
'A dove to fly across the face (of),' to change colour; to be or become confounded; to look blank or astounded
(-ke), To get warm or angry in the presence (of); to behave disrespectfully in the presence (of a superior)
To bring to the lips; to utter, say, speak of, tell, relate
To place a seal on the lips (of, -apne, or uske, &c.), to seal the lips
'To be without a nose on the face'; to be shameless
(or hawāʼiyāṅ) uṛnā, or phirnā, or phir-jānā, or ćhuṭnā, 'To have a squib or rocket discharged against one's face'; to be or become confounded; to change colour; to look blank or astonished
(kisī-ke), To fall into the mouth (of); — to be eaten; — to behave with boldness or presumption (before)
adj. f. (m. -paṛā), On the tongues (of people; — syn. zabān-zad)
v.n. To open the mouth wide, to gape (with wonder, &c.)
adj. & s.m. Outspoken, fearless, unconcerned; foul-mouthed, abusive; — an outspoken, or fearless, &c. person; — a vain babbler
v.n. The face to be turned or distorted, the face to be paralyzed; — the face to be turned away (from, -se), to be satiated or cloyed; to be disgusted (with); to be displeased (with)
To distend the cheeks; to make a wry face, &c. (i.q. muṅh bigāṛnā, q.v.)
'To burst open the lips and speak'; to break silence; to make bold to say
(-se, or -kī t̤araf-se), To turn the face away (from); to be averse (to); — to abstain (from); to avoid, shun
To gape (i.q. muṅh bānā, and muṅh pasārnā), to yawn; — to desire much; — to presume; — to demand a high price (for); to raise one's price
(-kā), To gaze, or stare, at the face (of); to be astonished, or afflicted; to look blank, or foolish
Just look at his face! what assurance! what cheek! — muṅh toṛnā (-kā), To break the mouth (of); — to harass
muṅh thutānā or thuthānā = muṅh banānā
(-kā), To slap the face (of), to box the ears (of)
To distort the face; to make wry faces (see muṅh bigāṛnā)
'To join face (to face),' to draw close together and whisper; to talk scandal in an under-tone
(-kā), To lick the face (of); to caress; to make much (of), to curry favour (with), to toady
(-kā), To make mouths or faces (at), to mock; — to make a bad imitation (of), to caricature
adj. Attached (to), intimate (with); possessing the ear (of), having an ascendancy (over)
(-ke), To be or become familiar or intimate (with); to attach oneself (to); — to face, confront (with hostile intention)
To bite, to snap (as a horse); to open the mouth (to devour); — to work the mouth or jaws, to eat
The mouth to be moving or working; to chew the cud; — to be foul-mouthed
adj. Sheepish, shamefaced, bashful, timid; — keeping at home, not visiting friends
(-se), To hide or veil the face (from bashfulness, &c.); to keep parda; — to be bashful; — to be in hiding, to hide, skulk; — to avoid, shun, to evade
s.m. & f. One who hides his (or her) face; — a bashful person; — a skulker, &c.; — blind-man's buff
(-kā), To touch the face (of); — to give (one) a formal invitation (to)
adv. Face to face, before, in the presence (of)
(-ko), To show one's face (to), to appear before (one) with confidence and satisfaction (e.g. kyā muṅh dikhāʼūṅgā? 'how can I show my face?')
s.f. A present given (by the female relations of the bridegroom, and the friends of the family) on the first visit to a bride
(-ke, &c.), To continue staring at (one) with surprise, to be lost in astonishment
To say pleasant things (to), to flatter

(apnā, or kisī-kā, &c.), To see one's face (in a glass); — to look at the face (of); to look about idly; — to stare at in astonishment or helplessness; — to look to (one, for aid, &c.), to depend (on); — to have a regard (for one)
or prīt, or ćāh, or maḥabbat, Show of regard or friendship before a person; apparent friendship or affection
muṅh-dekhī bāt kahnā = muṅh dekh-kar bāt kahnā
(-meṅ), To put the mouth or lips (into, or to); — to make familiarized, to favour, to countenance
(-meṅ), To put the mouth (into, or to); to thrust the muzzle (into); to eat; — to bite (a horse); to peck or seize with the beak (a cock, in fighting); — to beg, request
To keep a good countenance; to keep on good terms (with)
adj. Verbal (message, or evidence, &c.), oral, vivâvoce
'The face to become yellow,' to turn pale (from fear)
Hard-mouthed (a horse); restive; — headstrong, stubborn, obstinate; — outspoken, &c. (i.q. muṅh-phaṭ, q.v.)
s.f. Hardness of mouth; headstrongness, &c.; — foul-mouthedness, abusiveness
To puff out the cheeks; to pout, to sulk; — to cause the face (of another, -kā, &c.) to swell (by a slap, or blow; — i.q. muṅh phulānā, and muṅh bigāṛnā)
To contract the face, to make a wry face
To bridle the mouth or tongue; to hold one's tongue
The mouth to become dry or parched; — the face to become thin or emaciated
(-ke, or mere, &c.), To take the words out of the mouth (of); to anticipate (and to prevent from speaking) one who is on the point of speaking
Flowers (of rhetoric) to fall from the lips (of, -ke); — (ironic.) to rate in round terms, to reproach, to abuse
Milk to drop from the mouth (of); to be yet a child, to have no sense
(-kā), To sew up the mouth (of); — to close or seal the lips (of, i.q. muṅh-par mǒhr karnā); to silence (a person); — to give hush-money (to, i.q. muṅh bharnā)
(-kā), To change colour, to turn pale (esp. from fear)
To blacken one's own face; to incur disgrace; — to blacken the face (of another, -kā); to disgrace, bring disgrace on; to punish; — to turn out with disgrace; to break off all connection (with)
The face to be or become black or blackened; to incur disgrace, be disgraced
muṅh-kā laććhan jhaṛnā = muṅh-kī loʼī utarnā
'With the mouth's (morsel) in the mouth and the hand's (spoonful) in the hand'; staggered, aghast
s.m. A morsel; — anything easily attained

(-kī t̤araf, or kī or), To turn the face (to or towards), to direct the steps (towards); to face, confront; — to attack; to abuse; — to regard with favour, be partial (to); — to direct the attention (to), to be greedy or miserly; to come to a head, to burst or, open (as a boil, or an abscess, &c.); — to make the first attempt at seizing game (a young hound, or other animal used in hunting)
(-ke), To get a taste for blood; vice to grow (on one)
The mouth to become open; — to become foul-mouthed or abusive
(-kā), To cause (one's) mouth to become opened; to make (or to provoke one to) speak
To open the mouth; — to abuse; — to uncover the face; to present to view; to reveal
(or bhal) girnā, To fall on the face, or face foremost
muṅh-ke kawwe uṛ-jānā = muṅh-par fāḵẖta uṛ-jānā
To eat one's own words; to be convicted out of one's own mouth; — to be browbeaten; — to receive a blow on the face; — to suffer disgrace; to be put to the blush
(-ke), To plug the mouth (of), to gag; to silence; — to tie the tongue (of a person) with a charm or spell; — to exorcise
or jānā, To lose (one's) brightness of countenance, or (one's) good looks; — to lose the sense of shame; — to lose (one's) good manners, to become corrupted, or depraved
To redden the mouth or lips; — to make the face (of another, -kā) red (by slapping)
v.n. The mouth or lips to be tinged with red; — the face to become red, or to flush (with anger, &c.); — to be honoured, to get a good name
'To muffle up the face and lie down'; to lie down in the sulks; to take to one's bed in grief
To hang down the face or head; — to make a long face, to be down in the mouth
To apply the mouth or lips (to, -meṅ)

(-ke, or mere, &c.), To have the mouth burned or affected (by a pungent substance, &c.); — to suit the mouth or palate (of), to be agreeable to the taste (of); — to become used, or accustomed (to), or familiarized (with); — to become intimate (with), to become a favourite (of); — to dispute, or bandy words (with)
'To attack (one) with the mouth,' to bite; to sting; to say biting or stinging things; — to attack, to fall upon; — to stop the mouth (of a person, -kā); to silence; — to feed
or muṅh-māgā, adj. (f. -ī), Demanded, requested, or asked for by word of mouth
s.m. Price demanded or asked for; — muṅh-māṅgī murād, s.f. Any thing or object obtained in accordance with expressed wish; — heart-felt wish
s.f. The death asked or wished for
To make a wry face; — to turn up the nose; — to frown, to be displeased, or offended

(-se), To turn away the face (from); to disregard; to be averse (to); to avoid, shun; to abstain or desist (from); — to turn (against), to rebel, revolt; — to turn a deaf ear (to); to give the cold shoulder (to)
(-kā), To sweeten the mouth (of); to give (one) a treat; — to give a sop or bribe (to)
The mouth to water; to feel eager desire (for); — to be tempted, or seduced
(-ke), To drop water into the mouth (of); — to perform the last offices (for)
To read to oneself, to mumble
To take a straw in one's mouth (as a sign of humility, or submission); to humble or abase oneself, to lick the dust; — to submit (to), resign oneself (to)
s.m.f.(?) The mouth-piece of a ḥuqqa
To look blank or foolish; — to simper
v.n. Not to have the face (to)
v.n. The cheeks to sink, &c. (i.q. muṅh utar-jānā, q.v.)
'The face and hands to break'; the limbs to be broken; to meet with a bad accident
To wash the face and hands
To return (from any enterprise) with a face (expressive of disappointment) peculiarly one's own; to return disappointed; to fail of success
To put the hand to the mouth (in admiration, or astonishment, or shame) and remain motionless; to be astonished, or ashamed, &c.
or ḵẖāk tere muṅh-meṅ, Dirt in your mouth! may your mouth be fouled!
(May his, or your,) face (become) black! may disgrace overtake him!

more: n. m. S. मुख Pr. muṅh, muham . 1. Face; countenance. 1. Merī mere muṅh par, terī tere muṅh par . Prov. With me he is for me, with you he is for you. One mouth two (contradictory) statements. 2. Āg kahte muṅh nahīṅ jaltā . Prov. To say 'fire' wont burn you. 3. Opening; orifice; entrance; hole. 1. Pājāme kā muṅh . 2. Zakhm kā muṅh . 3. Lālā, gon kā moṅḍhā karo! Sir, open your sack! 4. Makān kā muṅh baṛā sakṛā hai . 4. Presence ( huzūrī , 1). Muṅh se mahābā . Prov. The master's presence keeps the servants in awe. 5. Regard; esteem ( lihāz ). 1. Kutte! terā …

def: s.m. corr. of A. munīb, q.v. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'
more: n. m. Cor. of A. munīb . 1. A Hindī accountant or clerk. 2. ( munīm dīvān ) An agent; a manager. munīm'ī , n. f. The office of a munīm .

subs: v.t. To divide (a thing, into, -par); to apportion

ety: act. part. of اِنْقطع 'to be broken, cut off, or detached,' &c., vii (and quasi-pass.
def: part. adj. Cut off, broken off; separated, disjoined; abrupt; interrupted; finished, concluded, terminated; ceased; settled, decided; — decisive, conclusive; — exterminated; extinct
more: adj. 1. Broken or cut off. 2. Terminated; finished. 3. Settled; decided. 4. Extinct. 5. Conclusive ( chuktā ).

subs: v.t. To cut short, break off, or stop (at, -par), to conclude, &c.
v.n. To be cut off; to be cut short (at, -par); to be finished, or terminated, &c.; to terminate, to cease, to fail; to become extinct.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُنْقَطِع (munqati'), from Arabic مُنْقَطِع (munqaṭiʕ).

ety: act. part. of اِنْقتل 'to be slain,' vii (and quasi-pass.

more: n. m. 1. One who denies. 2. An atheist (S. nāstik ). munkir nakīr , n. m. ( Mah .) Two angels who examine the spirits of the departed in the tomb. munkir h. , v. n. 1. To deny; unsay. 2. To disown; renounce.

part. act. (IV. of نکر Heb. and Aram. ) Denying, rejecting; one who denies, denier. منکرنکير Munkar Nakīr, The names of two angels, who, agreeably to the Muhammadan creed, are supposed to examine the spirits of the departed in the tomb. منکرهونا munkir honā, To deny.
subs: (-kā), To deny; disavow; to retract (one's word); to disown, to renounce.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُنْكِر (munkir).

def: s.f. Puss-puss (a mode of calling a cat).
more: Puss puss, a mode of calling a cat.

def: s.m. Tares; a black and bitter grain which grows with wheat.
more: n. m. A weed which grows up with wheat.

def: v.n. = موننا mūnnā, q.v. s.m. (f. -ī), Darling, pet.

def: v.n. To be silent; — to forget.

more: n. m. munnī , n. f. Darling.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀼𑀁𑀟 (muṃḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit मुण्ड (muṇḍa). Cognate with Punjabi ਮੁੰਡਾ (muṇḍā), ਮੁੰਨਾ (munnā).

def: s.m. = مونڐ mūṅḍ, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Shaved, bald, having no hair on the head; uncovered; — destitute of horns (an ox, or a ram), hornless (i.q. muṇḍā, q.v.); — stripped of top-leaves or branches, lopped (as a tree); pointless, blunt; (fig.) dull, obtuse; — low, mean; — s.m. A shaven head, a bald pate; a skull; — the forehead; — the head; — head, headman, chief, principal (i.q. murh, q.v.); — a tree stripped of its top branches, the trunk of a lopped tree; a pollard; — a barber; — an epithet of Rāhu; — name of a Daitya
subs: (˚ḍa+ak˚), s.m. Headless letters, the Nāgarī character without the top line (as written by the Kāyaths; see kaithi, and muṛiyā)
s.m. A sect of mendicants who extort alms by threatening to split (or wound) their heads, &c.; an individual of that sect; an extortioner
s.m. The action, or the quality, of a muṇḍ-cirā; extortion

s.f. A posture in which the legs are drawn up and the head is thrust either between them, or into some corner or recess, or under some covering or covert; crouching or contracted form of the body and limbs
To lie with the head between the legs, &c.; to lie round with the knees to the stomach, and the head between them, or resting on them
or muṇḍ-mālā, s.f. A necklace or string of human skulls.

Brij. Rus. Pretence. mūṅṛā k . See bahāna k .
v.t. = muṅḍānā, q.v. v.t. To shave (i.q. muṅṛānā).

= muṛiyā, q.v. s.m. A bald-head, a shaveling, a contemptuous term for a gosāṅʼīṅ, or a bairāgī (because they shave their heads wholly); — the Devanāgarī character written without the upper line (see kaithī).
part. adj. = mūṅḍlā, q.v.
v.n. = muṇḍnā, q.v. v.n. To be shut, or closed; to be filled up, be blocked up; to be contracted; — v.t. To close, &c. ( = mūṅdnā, q.v.)
v.t. = mūṅḍnā, q.v. v.n. To be shut, or closed; to be filled up, be blocked up; to be contracted; — v.t. To close, &c. ( = mūṅdnā, q.v.)
s.m. corr. of موش mūsh, q.v. s.f. Stealing, robbing; theft.

s.m. = موسل mūsal, q.v. s.m. A long, heavy, wooden pestle used in clearing rice from the husk (vulg. mūslā, q.v.); a pounder; — a club, mace; — name of the twenty-second yoga or division of the moon's path
subs: s.m. 'The bearer of the club,' an epithet of Baladev; — ʻclub-stream,' very heavy rain

def: part. adj. Being drawn (into, as one thing into another); threaded, strung (as beads, or pearls, &c.); enrolled (in); joined; added, appended, annexed; filed (as papers)
more: adj. سلک thrusting in. 1. Threaded; strung together. 2. Appended; annexed. munsalik k. , v. a. To append; file. munsalak-shudah , A. P. adj. Attached; filed.
(part. act. VII. of سلک) adj. Threaded, strung (as, pearls), being drawn (one thing in another).
subs: v.t. To draw on or into (-meṅ); to thread, &c.
part. Strung; attached; appended, annexed; filed.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُنْسَلِكْ (munsalik).

def: part. adj. = munsalik, q.v. part. adj. Being drawn (into, as one thing into another); threaded, strung (as beads, or pearls, &c.); enrolled (in); joined; added, appended, annexed; filed (as papers)
subs: v.t. To draw on or into (-meṅ); to thread, &c.
part. Strung; attached; appended, annexed; filed.

def: part. adj. Turned back; averted; converted; receding, departing, deviating; — rebellious; — (in Gram.) inflected, declined, declinable

ety: act. part. of اِنْصرم 'to be cut, or cut off; to end, cease,' &c., vii (and quasi-pass.
def: s.m. A manager, an administrator; — a subordinate settlement-officer; — head clerk (of a court of settlement, or of a judge's court); — a substitute, proxy; — teacher of a school of Paṭwārīs.
more: n. m. 1. Manager; adminstrator ( sarbarāhkār , 1). 2. A subordinate settlement officer. 3. The head clerk of a court of settlement or judge's court. 4. A proxy; substitute. 5. The teacher of a school who teaches Paṭwārīs .

ety: act. part. of انشأ 'to create, or produce; to compose,' &c., iv of نشأ 'to grow, to spring up,' &c.

more: n. m. نشاء growing, increasing. 1. A writer; scribe; scriviner. 2. ( Europeans ) A Persian teacher. 3. A title of respect. munshī-khānā , n. m. The vernacular office room. munshī-garī , n. f. 1. A clerkship. 2. A teachership. 3. Good writing or composition. mīr-munshī , n. m. The head of a Persian or vernacular office. munshiyānā khat , n. m. Good penmanship.

(part. act. IV. of نشأ Heb. ) s. m. A writer, amanuensis, a secretary. (In India, among Europeans, the word commonly means) A tutor, a teacher of Persian or Hindustani, a language-master. منشي گري munshī-garī, s. f. The business of a munshī, secretariship.
subs: s.m. The room for native scribes in an Indian office
s.f. Business of a munshī; secretaryship; clerkship; teachership; — good composition; good writing
s.m. The head of the Persian or vernacular department of an office.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian منشی (munšī), from Arabic مُنْشِئ (munšiʔ). Cognate with Bengali মুনশি (munśi).

def: adj. Of or relating to a munshī; munshī-like, clerk-like
subs: s.m. Writing like a munshī's, good handwriting or penmanship.

more: n. m. نصف half. 1. A just judge. 2. A subordinate judge.
(part. act. IV. of نصف) adj. 1. Equitable, just, fair. 2. (subst.) A distributer of justice, a judge, an arbitrator. منصف مزاج munṣif-mizāj, Just in temperament, (a person) of propriety or taste.
subs: adj. Just-minded, just in temperament; discreet; (a person) of propriety or taste.

ety: munṣif, q.v.+āna = Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. Equitably, justly, fairly, candidly.

def: s.f. Justice, equity; judgment, decision, arbitration; — office or duty of a munṣif; — the court of a Munṣif; — adj. Of or relating to a Munṣif
more: n. f. 1. Justice; equity. 2. A Munsif's Court. munsifī k. , v. a. 1. To preside over a court of justice. 2. To do justice. munsifā'nah , adv. Equitably; justly.
s. f. Arbitration, judgment, decision, justice.
subs: To follow the profession of a Munṣif; — to preside over a court of justice; — to do justice, to judge or decide equitably.

def: s.m. = موسلا mūslā, q.v. s.m. Public or exemplary punishment, an example.

subs: (-par), To put a cover (on), to cover; to place or fit (upon); to apply (to)
(-par), To cover, to fit (upon); to be (on or upon); to coincide, or coalesce (with).

more: (part. act. VIII. of نفي) adj. Dissipated, destroyed, separated.

more: (part. act. VII. of طفأ Chald. and Syr. ) adj. Extinguished.

def: adj. Proficient, perfect (scholar).

def: part. adj. Brought forth; — drawn out from; deduced, inferred, concluded; — s.m. That which is to be concluded, or inferred; conclusion, inference; consequence.
more: (part. pass. or act. IV. of نتج) adj. Deduced; (subst.) A consequence.

ety: pass. part. of اِنْتقل 'to be transported,' &c., viii of نقل 'to transport,' &c.

more: adj. نقل transporting. Transferred; transported. muntaqal-alaih , n. m. A transferee; an alienee; assignee. muntaqal k. , v. a. 1. To transfer; alienate; assign. 2. To dispose of; sell. 3. To endorse. muntaqal karnā bād sakārne ke , To negotiate after acceptance.
(part. pass. VIII. of نقل) adj. Transported, carried.
subs: s.m. A transferee; an alienee; an assignee
v.t. To transfer; to alienate; to assign; to part with, dispose of, get rid of; — to endorse

more: (part. act. VIII. of نقش) adj. Painted, carved, engraved.

more: adj. نخب extracting, drawing. 1. Chosen; selected. 2. Choice; rare. muntakhab, muntakhabāt , n. m. 1. Abstract; compendium. 2. A selection. 3. One of the gentry. Yeh muntakhab ādmī hai. muntakhab k. , v. a. To make an abstract or selection. muntakhab h. , v. n. To be chosen.
(part. pass. VIII. of نخب) adj. Chosen, selected; (subst.) A selection.

more: part. act. (VIII. of نقم Heb. and Aram. ) Taking revenge, avenging; avenger.

ety: act. or pass. part. of اِنْتشر 'to be spread, or scattered,' &c., viii of نشر 'to spread,' &c.

(part. act. or pass. VIII. of نشر Heb. and Aram. Chald. ) adj. Published, divulged, explained, spread, dispersed, diffused, diffuse.

ety: act. part. of اِنْتظم 'to be ordered, or arranged,' &c., viii of نظم 'to join, string; to order, arrange,' &c.

more: n. m. نظم strung. 1. A manager; superintendent. 2. An economist.
(part. pass. VIII. of نظم) adj. Ordered, arranged, disposed, adjusted.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian منتظم (muntazim), from Arabic مُنْتَظِم (muntaẓim), active participle of اِنْتَظَمَ (intaẓama).

ety: act. part. of اِنْتظر 'to expect; to wait anxiously for,' viii of نظر 'to see, look at, view,' &c.

(part. act. VIII. of نظر Heb. and Aram. and ) adj. Expecting with impatience, tarrying for, looking out for; subst. expectant.
subs: v.t. To keep (one) in expectation, to keep (one) waiting
(-kā), To watch (for), look out (for), to expect; to wait (for), to await.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = mūṛ, or mūṅṛ, qq.v.
s.m. corr. of مول mūl, q.v. adv. (dialec.) At last; after all; — in short; — at all, in the least; — but, however (e.g. ham-ne to bahuterā kahā, mul us-ne na mānā).

def: n. m. S. मूर्ख A stupid fellow (احمق). 1. Chātur ko chaugunā, mūrakh ko sau gunā . Prov. The clever fourfold, the fool a hundredfold. (Wrong estimates). 2. Phūle phale na beṅt jadapī sudhā barkīṅ jalad, Rāmāyan. Mūrakh hirde na chet jo gurū milahīṅ Biranch sam . The cane no fruit will yield though nectar it rained free, The fool a fool will be though Brahm his priest should be. 3. Hāṅge choran pāg utārī, mārakh kahve laī udhārī . Rus. The thief by force his turban took away, The fool says it's a loan he will repay. 4. ' Yeh mūrakh ḍāṅgar kiskā jo ṭaṭṭaṛ toṛe merā?' 'Hāṅk de, bīvī, hāṅk de, yeh mūrakh ḍāngar merā, Girī, chhuāre, chhoṛ-ke bhus khāne āyā terā .' 'Whose foolish wanton calf is this abreaking mat-door mine?' 'Drive, drive him, lady, away! the foolish calf is mine, He leaves my nuts and dates to browse on straw of thine.' (The question is put by a woman whose house a strange man attemped to enter, and the answer is made by the wife of the faithless husband). mūrakh-panā , n. m. mūrakhtāī , n. f. See احمقاپن
more: adj. Foolish, ignorant, stupid, idiot, fool. مورکهـ گانٿهـ mūrakh-gānṭh, The insisting of an ignorant fellow upon what he says, stubbornness.
wety: From Sanskrit मूर्ख (mūrkha).

def: part. adj. Dead, defunct; — weak, infirm; old decrepit; — s.m. A dead body, a corpse
more: adj. 1. Dead. 2. Weak; infirm; old; decrepit. Ajī! woh to murdah hai. murdah , n. m. A corpse ( murdār , 1). Khalqat murdah-pasaṅd hai . Prov. Men like men dead. (They are praised after death). murdah jalānā . Burning the dead; cremation. murdah-dil . Dispirited; dejected; sad. murdah-shū . One who washes the dead. murdah-farosh , Mah. n. m. A low caste whose business it is to carry the dead. murdah k. , v. a. To do to death; to kill. 1. Do hī din ke bukhār ne murdah kar dīyā . 2. Bar-bas ān-ke gāë pachhāṛat, Galā kāṭ jab āp līyā Jīvte ko murdā kar ḍārat, Vā ko kahe halāl kīyā . Kabīr. An ox upon his back by force to lay, To cut his throat and thus the ox to slay, To make a carcase of a living thing A lawful act to call—astonishing! murde kā māl , n. m. Effects of the deceased, generally sold cheap; hence, a cheap bargain. Tum to murde kā māl ḍhūṅḍte ho, saudā bane to kyoṅ-kar bane . (Sa…
1. adj. Dead. 2. s. m. A corpse, a dead body. مرده شو murda-sho, One who washes the dead. مرده فروش murda-farosh, A name of a cast whose business it is to carry the dead.
subs: adj. Dead-hearted, dead, lifeless; cold-hearted, cold; dispirited, dejected, sad
s.m. A washer of the dead
s.m. Name of a low caste whose business it is to carry the dead; a man of that caste
v.t. To render dead or defunct, to do to death, to lay low, to kill, to make a corpse of
s.m. Property of a dead person; effects of the deceased; — a cheap bargain (so termed because the effects of a dead person are generally sold cheap).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُرْدَه (murda). Doublet of مَرِت (marit).

def: part. adj. & s.m. corr. of مرده murda, q.v.

s.m. pl. (of murda), The dead.

ety: mur˚, fr. Zend rt. mar (mori); S. मर् (मृ);+dan+tan = S. aff. तं
def: v.n. & s.m. To die; dying; death.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मर्दन (mardana).

ety: fr. murdan
def: adj. To die; fit to die, deserving of death; — of or relating to a death; — death-like; — s.f. A death; a funeral, funeral obsequies; death-like stillness (syn. mautā)
more: n. f. 1. A death. 2. Funeral obsequies. 3. ( mautā ) Death-like stillness. murdanī chhānā, yā phirnā , v. n. To become deadly pale. murdanī yā maute meṅ jānā , v. n. To attend a funeral.
adj. Fit to die, to die.
subs: (-par), or murdanī phirnā (-par), Death-like pallor to come (over or upon), to become deadly pale
(-kī), To go to (or to attend) the funeral (of).
wety: From Sanskrit.

ety: mur(dan), q.v.+dār = tār = Pehl. tar = Zend tar = S. तार्
def: s.m. A dead body, a corpse; a carcass; — carrion; a piece of carrion; — adj. Dead; impure, unclean, polluted; dirty; squalid; ugly; profane; obscene; — intj. You carrion! wretch! vile creature!
more: adj. 1. Dead. Pāoṅ kī khāl murdār ho gaī . 2. ( Mah .) Unlawful; impure; profane. Sūar murdār hai. murdār , n. m. 1. A dead body; a corpse. 2. Carrion ( Mah .). The carcase of an animal that has died a natural death, or which has not been killed in a lawful manner, and is therefore unfit for food. murdār , Wom. intj. Wretch! hussy! Chal, murdār! mere mūṅh na lag! murdār-saṅg ; Pop. murdā-saṅg , n. m. Litharge. murdār'ī , Mah. Wom. n. f. A lizard. Shaikh sāhab kī haiṅ donoṅ abrūeṅ paivastah yūṅ Naghat Sir milā-kar jis tarah laṛtī haiṅ do murdāriyāṅ .
adj. Polluted, impure, squalid, ugly, profane; (subst.) Carrion, piece of carrion. مردارخوار murdār-ḵẖẉār, A species of eagle or crow that feeds filthily.
subs: s.m. An eater of carrion; a carrion-crow; a vulture
vulg. murdā-sang, s.m. Dross of lead; protoxide of lead, litharge.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مردار (murdār).

def: s.f. A kind of lizard.

ety: old P. murgh, muru; Pehl. mūrū; Zend meregha; S. मृग
def: s.m. A bird, fowl; a cock (com. murg̠ā in India)
more: n. m. Z. meregha, murveh, muruk . 1. A fowl; a bird. 2. ( murgā ) A cock. 1. Woh hī murge kī ek ṭāṅg . Prov. The fowl has only one leg. ☞ The servants of great men have a great contempt for the gullibility of their masters whom they cheat and plunder. The story goes that a great man had once a roast fowl with only one leg set before him, his cook protesting that the fowl belonged to a breed that had but one leg; and, the next day, he drew his master's attention to a fowl standing on a dunghill on one leg, saying 'there's another.' But the master cried out ' hush ,' and the fowl flew away, displaying both legs. 'Well' said the servant, 'if you had said hush to the other fowl he would have shown another leg likewise.' 2. Sun, bārī ke murgalā! tohe gerūṅ marvāë! Jab mero bālam ras bharo, jab hī āṅgan bolo āë . Wom. Rus. song. Now listen, cock, your throat will just be cut, I'd have you know…
s. m. A fowl, bird, cock (particularly, house-fowl). مرغ خانگي murg̠ẖ-i-ḵẖāngī, or خانگي مرغ ḵẖāngī murg̠ẖ, The domestick cock or hen. مرغ عيسيٰ murg̠ẖ-i-īﻌsạ̄, The bat.
subs: s.f. A water-fowl; a wild duck; a teal; — the plant Sanseviera zeylanica
s.m. (dialec.) The tow, or fibrous or filamentous stuff, prepared from the murg̠ābī plant, of which the pack-thread used by matmen, &c., is usually made
s.m. A cock-fancier; a cock-fighter; one who feeds cocks
(or ḵẖāngī murg̠), s.m. & f. A domestic (or barn-door) fowl
s.m. The morning bird, the cock; — the nightingale

ety: murg̠, q.v.+Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A hen
more: n. f. A hen. murgī-kā , A whorson. murgī kā gū , A worthless thing. murgī kī azān , n. f. The crowing of a hen. Murgī kī azāṅ aur aurat kī gavāhī kā etabār nahīṅ . The hen's crow and a woman's word no one trusts. murgī mejar , ( Fac .) One who deals in hens. murgī-vālā , n. m. 1. A poulterer. 2. An abuse. anḍe murgī kī bahs, lit . reasoning as to which was first, the hen or the egg. Reasoning in a circle. chīn kī murgī , n. f. A China hen; guinea fowl. sulemān murgī ; H. dhan churī , The Malabar pied hornbill; Hydrocissa coronata .
s. f. A hen. مرغي والا murg̠ẖī-wālā, s. m. A poulterer.
subs: s.m. (f. -wālī), A poulterer; — a term of abuse
'The discussion about the egg and the hen' (i.e. as to which was first); reasoning in a circle
The Malabar pied hornbill, Hydrocissa coronata (syn. dhan-ćurī).

def: part. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Stupefied, bewildered, perplexed, confounded, confused; — infatuated; blinded by passion, or by ignorance; stupid, dull, silly, foolish, simple, ignorant, brutish; — a fool, blockhead, dolt, idiot, simpleton; a sluggard
subs: (˚ṛha+āt˚), or mūṛh-man, adj. & s.m. & f. Bewildered or stupefied in mind; insensible; stupid in mind; having the disposition of a fool; stupid, foolish; — a stupid person, a fool (i.q. mūṛh).

s. m. See مونڐ mūnḍ.
adj. Foolish, stupid, ignorant, brutal; (subst.) Fool, simpleton. موڙه من muṛh-man, Stupid in mind.
